# sdrf-annotated-datasets (LLM / tool context)

> Canonical repository of SDRF (Sample and Data Relationship Format) annotations
> for public proteomics datasets (ProteomeXchange and related accessions). The
> format specification and templates live elsewhere; this repo holds curated
> `.sdrf.tsv` files for community-driven re-annotation and reuse.

## Purpose

- Advance **community annotations** with participation from annotators, the **SDRF
  core team**, and **ProteomeXchange** stakeholders.
- Enable **re-annotation** of public proteomics data with consistent,
  machine-validated metadata.
- Keep high-churn annotation work **separate** from the specification repository:
  https://github.com/bigbio/proteomics-sample-metadata

## Key paths in this repository

- README.md — Mission, layout, validation, agentic best practices
- CONTRIBUTING.md — PR workflow, local validation, expectations for agent-assisted work
- LICENSE — Apache License 2.0
- datasets/{ACCESSION}/*.sdrf.tsv — Dataset annotations (one folder per accession)
- .github/workflows/validate-sdrf.yml — CI: `parse_sdrf validate-sdrf` on changed files

## Authoritative specification and tooling

- SDRF-Proteomics specification (AsciiDoc, templates, guidelines):
  https://github.com/bigbio/proteomics-sample-metadata
- Validator / CLI (`parse_sdrf`), installed from source in CI:
  https://github.com/bigbio/sdrf-pipelines

## When proposing edits

1. Read CONTRIBUTING.md and README.md (agentic section).
2. Only add or change files under `datasets/` unless explicitly asked to update docs or CI.
3. Validate with `parse_sdrf validate-sdrf --sdrf_file <path> --use_ols_cache_only`.
4. In pull request descriptions, link to the public dataset record and state whether output was human-reviewed.

## Migration note

Discussion that motivated splitting annotated projects from the spec repo:
https://github.com/bigbio/proteomics-sample-metadata/issues/817
